category_title: Attention-seeking students without language disorders who mimic peers
student_description: Sidney is a kindergarten student who seeks peer attention by spontaneously yelling out answers and mimicking her classmates during discussions. She uniquely requires explicit reminders to raise her hand to manage her impulsivity, as well as specific prompts to connect vocabulary to her own home life to encourage authentic elaboration rather than copying peers.

criteria.rationale: While Sidney initially needs heavy prompting for behavior and personal connections, she needs this scaffolding to decrease over time to build independent vocabulary use.
criteria.check: The tutor's long-term plan must explicitly describe a strategy for gradually reducing behavioral reminders or instructional support over time to build independence.
